questions.sentence: Cognitive Load Theory is a fundamental psychological principle used in UX design to explain how much mental effort is required to use a website
The goal of a great user interface is to minimize extraneous cognitive load the unnecessary mental effort caused by poor design confusing navigation or excessive information
When the cognitive load is too high users become frustrated and are likely to abandon the task

Senior developers and designers use principles like Hick's Law which states that the time it takes to make a decision increases with the number of options and Progressive Disclosure showing only the necessary information at any given time to keep the experience smooth
By understanding the psychological limitations of the human brain you can design more intuitive accessible and user friendly products that successfully guide users toward their goals
